title: charpentiera elliptica
intro: Charpentiera elliptica, also known as the 'Black Sandalwood', is a type of flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Hawaii and Tahiti. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and extracts. The health benefits of Charpentiera elliptica include relief from skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Charpentiera elliptica include essential oil, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
